Title: Echinochloa crus-galli genome analysis provides insight into its adaptation and invasiveness as a weed

Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful target for reducing barnyardgrass allelopathic competitiveness; conversely, rice improvement may focus on DIMBOA resistance loci rather than deployment of this weed gene. If manipulating in model systems, stack with downstream BX genes because DIMBOA production depends on the cluster; caution that enhancing allelochemical production may increase weediness or non-target effects.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Converts indole-3-glycerol phosphate to indole, initiating benzoxazinoid/DIMBOA biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes DIMBOA accumulation in E. crus-galli, contributing to inhibition of rice growth during co-cultivation.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Comparative genomics/synteny with maize BX cluster; RNA-seq showing co-cultivation-induced upregulation of cluster genes; HPLC quantification of increased DIMBOA under co-culture; exogenous DIMBOA bioassay inhibiting rice growth.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies the first committed precursor for target product biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Co-cultivation with rice cultivar PI312777; sampled at 3 h for RNA-seq and 45 min/1.5 h/3 h for DIMBOA measurement.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: DIMBOA-cluster BX1 copies were significantly upregulated in E. crus-galli during co-cultivation with rice relative to mono-culture.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Genome assembly and annotation; RNA-seq and differential expression analysis, NCBI BioProject PRJNA268892; SRA SRR5920284-SRR5920293, SRR5902661, SRR5903813-SRR5903830, SRR5903559-SRR5903564
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Three syntenic copies of the DIMBOA cluster were identified in the hexaploid E. crus-galli genome; two copies likely originated from E. oryzicola and one from the unknown diploid ancestor.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Potential ecological tradeoff inferred: greater allelopathic capacity may alter interspecific interactions; no direct growth penalty reported.
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: BX1/indole-3-glycerol phosphate lyase; tryptophan synthase alpha-like family
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Committed-step enzyme; key control status not directly established in this paper.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. BX1 is part of three DIMBOA biosynthetic gene-cluster copies found in E. crus-galli. 2. BX1 expression increased when barnyardgrass was co-cultivated with rice. 3. Induction of the DIMBOA cluster correlated with elevated DIMBOA content and rice growth inhibition, supporting a causal role in allelopathy.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Whole barnyardgrass seedlings/root exudate extracts during co-culture
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Allelopathic suppression of neighboring plants; defense compound.

Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target to weaken barnyardgrass allelopathy; should be considered together with the full BX cluster because single-gene changes may not fully block DIMBOA output.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Cytochrome P450-mediated oxidation of indole to indolin-2-one in early DIMBOA biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Supports DIMBOA biosynthesis and thereby barnyardgrass allelopathic activity against rice.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Cluster synteny with maize; RNA-seq induction during co-cultivation; metabolite profiling showing increased DIMBOA under the same condition.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms an early intermediate on the direct path to DIMBOA.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Most BX2 copies were induced by co-cultivation; BX2 in copy 2 was an exception noted by the authors.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Genome assembly plus RNA-seq, BioProject PRJNA268892
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Three cluster copies exist, but not every gene copy responded equally; BX2 in copy 2 was not significantly upregulated.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. BX2 is a member of the three-copy DIMBOA cluster in E. crus-galli. 2. Its expression generally increased during rice co-cultivation, consistent with activation of DIMBOA biosynthesis. 3. The response was copy-dependent, with BX2 in cluster copy 2 not strongly induced.

Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate metabolic engineering target for suppressing benzoxazinoid production in barnyardgrass; likely most effective when edited with other BX genes in the cluster.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Cytochrome P450-mediated oxidation step converting indolin-2-one to 3-hydroxyindolin-2-one.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Contributes to DIMBOA production associated with allelopathy toward rice.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Comparative genomics; co-cultivation RNA-seq; DIMBOA metabolite increase under the same treatment.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms a direct intermediate in the target-product pathway.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: BX3 copies were significantly upregulated under rice co-cultivation.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Genome assembly and RNA-seq, BioProject PRJNA268892
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Identified as part of each DIMBOA cluster copy syntenic with the maize BX region.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. BX3 was identified within the E. crus-galli DIMBOA cluster. 2. Its expression increased in response to neighboring rice. 3. Cluster induction paralleled increased DIMBOA accumulation and rice inhibition.

Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential node for disrupting barnyardgrass DIMBOA production; should be modified in combination with the rest of the cluster.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Supports DIMBOA biosynthesis and the weed's allelopathic effect on rice.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Synteny-based identification; RNA-seq induction during co-culture; HPLC confirmation of higher DIMBOA when the cluster is induced.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms the immediate benzoxazinone precursor of DIMBOA.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: BX4 copies were significantly induced by rice co-cultivation.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Genome assembly and RNA-seq, BioProject PRJNA268892
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Part of all three DIMBOA cluster copies.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. BX4 is a conserved member of the DIMBOA biosynthetic cluster in E. crus-galli. 2. It is induced by co-cultivation with rice. 3. Its pathway participation supports induced DIMBOA accumulation during allelopathic interaction.

Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate editing target to lower DIMBOA output and reduce weed competitiveness; suitable for cluster-level engineering strategies.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Glucosylates DIBOA to DIBOA-glucoside, enabling downstream conversion to DIMBOA-related products.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required in the cluster supporting DIMBOA accumulation associated with barnyardgrass allelopathy.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Comparative genomics/synteny; co-cultivation RNA-seq; correlated metabolite increase for DIMBOA.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms a direct pathway intermediate required before final hydroxylation/methoxylation to DIMBOA.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: BX5 copies were significantly upregulated in co-cultivated E. crus-galli.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Genome assembly and RNA-seq, BioProject PRJNA268892
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The E. crus-galli cluster showed synteny to maize BX1-5 and BX8; BX6 and BX7 were not described in the syntenic segment in this paper.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. BX5 was found in the E. crus-galli DIMBOA cluster. 2. It was induced under rice co-culture. 3. Its inclusion in the induced pathway supports the causal link between cluster activation and DIMBOA accumulation.

Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target for reducing DIMBOA production in barnyardgrass; should be engineered along with upstream BX genes for durable pathway suppression.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Final O-methylation step leading to DIMBOA formation from a hydroxylated benzoxazinone precursor/glucoside.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Directly contributes to DIMBOA biosynthesis linked to rice growth inhibition.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Synteny-based cluster identification; RNA-seq showing co-culture induction of most BX8 copies except copy 2; DIMBOA metabolite profiling and rice bioassay.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly forms the target product or its immediate final form.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Most BX8 copies were induced during co-culture; BX8 in copy 2 was an exception.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Genome assembly and RNA-seq, BioProject PRJNA268892
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Three cluster copies were identified; BX8 in copy 2 was not significantly upregulated.
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: DIMBOA (or DIMBOA-glucoside depending on substrate context)
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. BX8 is a terminal DIMBOA-pathway gene present in the E. crus-galli DIMBOA cluster. 2. Its expression was generally induced by rice co-cultivation, though one cluster copy was an exception. 3. Cluster activation coincided with elevated DIMBOA levels and rice growth suppression.

Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate target for modifying barnyardgrass pathogen defense; if engineering disease resistance, should be combined with KSL4, MAS, and CYP99A pathway genes. Caution: boosting phytoalexin pathways can alter defense-growth balance.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Cyclizes geranylgeranyl diphosphate to syn-copalyl diphosphate, initiating the momilactone diterpenoid pathway.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Supports momilactone A biosynthesis implicated in resistance to Pyricularia oryzae rather than allelopathy during rice co-culture.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Synteny with rice momilactone cluster; phylogenetic analysis; qRT-PCR showing induction of cluster genes under P. oryzae infection for key members; infection assay on leaves.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies the committed diterpene precursor for target-product biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: P. oryzae infection of E. crus-galli leaves; not induced during rice co-cultivation.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Cluster is not expressed during co-cultivation with rice; the pathway is associated with induction under fungal infection, although direct qRT-PCR evidence in the paper was shown specifically for MAS and KSL4.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Genome assembly; transcriptome under co-culture; phylogenetic analysis; sequencing data under PRJNA268892
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Only one momilactone A cluster copy was found in E. crus-galli, unlike the three-copy DIMBOA cluster. E. oryzicola reads did not support its presence in the paternal donor.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Possible defense-growth tradeoff inferred but not experimentally measured.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Committed-step enzyme; rate limitation not established
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CPS4 is part of a single momilactone A gene cluster identified in E. crus-galli. 2. The cluster is not activated during barnyardgrass-rice co-culture. 3. The pathway is instead implicated in fungal defense, particularly against P. oryzae.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Leaves under pathogen challenge (inferred from infection experiments)

Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful target to manipulate phytoalexin-based disease resistance; likely most effective in multigene engineering with CPS4, CYP99A2, and MAS.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Converts syn-copalyl diphosphate to syn-pimaradiene (momilactone diterpene backbone intermediate).
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes momilactone A pathway flux associated with response to blast fungus infection.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Synteny with rice momilactone cluster; qRT-PCR demonstrating dramatic induction after P. oryzae infection; phylogenetic analysis.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms a direct hydrocarbon intermediate leading to momilactone A.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: P. oryzae infection strongly induced KSL4; not expressed during co-culture with rice.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: KSL4 transcripts increased dramatically, in some infected leaves by >10-fold after P. oryzae infection; absent/undetectable in co-culture transcriptome.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Genome assembly; co-culture transcriptome; qRT-PCR under pathogen infection; data under PRJNA268892
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: KSL4 from E. crus-galli clustered phylogenetically closer to Oryza orthologs than expected from species relationships, suggesting unusual evolutionary history.
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Class I diterpene synthase / kaurene synthase-like family
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. KSL4 is a core enzyme gene in the E. crus-galli momilactone A cluster. 2. It is not activated by rice co-cultivation but is strongly induced by P. oryzae infection. 3. This supports a role for the cluster in fungal defense rather than allelopathy in this species.

Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target for tuning disease-resistance metabolite production; likely requires pathway-level engineering. Caution that stronger phytoalexin accumulation may affect resource allocation.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Cytochrome P450 oxidation of syn-pimaradiene-derived intermediate in the oxidative branch toward momilactone A.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Supports momilactone A production implicated in antifungal defense in E. crus-galli.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Syntenic cluster identification and phylogenetic analysis; the cluster was linked to pathogen-responsive expression, though direct qRT-PCR in the paper was shown for MAS and KSL4.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Direct oxidative tailoring step toward target-product formation.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Associated with P. oryzae-induced cluster activation; not induced in rice co-culture transcriptome.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Momilactone cluster genes were not expressed during co-culture; infection-responsive role inferred for the cluster, but specific qRT-PCR evidence for CYP99A2 was not shown.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Genome assembly; co-culture transcriptome; phylogenetic analysis; PRJNA268892
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Unlike CPS4/KSL4/MAS, the CYP99A2 tree followed expected species phylogeny, clustering first with Setaria/Panicum orthologs.
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Diterpenoid phytoalexin pathway, oxidative tailoring branch
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CYP99A2 is one of four adjoining genes in the E. crus-galli momilactone A cluster. 2. The cluster was not activated during co-culture with rice. 3. The pathway is implicated instead in pathogen defense, with CYP99A2 providing an oxidative step toward momilactone A.

Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: High-value candidate for manipulating pathogen-inducible phytoalexin biosynthesis; a promising engineering target for disease resistance but may require co-optimization with upstream diterpene pathway genes.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Momilactone A synthase catalyzes the final oxidation/rearrangement step producing momilactone A from an oxidized diterpene precursor.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Directly promotes momilactone A formation and is strongly induced by P. oryzae infection, supporting a role in blast resistance.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Syntenic cluster identification; qRT-PCR after P. oryzae infection showing dramatic induction, in some leaves >10-fold; infection phenotype assay.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: P. oryzae infection of leaves strongly induced MAS; not expressed during co-cultivation with rice.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: MAS was dramatically induced after P. oryzae infection, with some infected leaves showing >10-fold increase; no expression in the co-culture transcriptome.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Genome assembly; RNA-seq under co-culture; qRT-PCR under pathogen infection; PRJNA268892
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Only one copy of the momilactone A cluster was found in E. crus-galli. Phylogenetically, MAS grouped unexpectedly close to Oryza orthologs.
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Diterpenoid phytoalexin pathway, terminal momilactone A branch
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Possible metabolic cost of defense induction not tested.
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: Oxidized diterpene precursor of momilactone A
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Terminal determinant supported for product formation; rate limitation not established
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MAS is a core terminal-pathway gene in the E. crus-galli momilactone A cluster. 2. MAS expression is strongly induced by P. oryzae infection but absent during rice co-culture. 3. This provides direct evidence that the cluster functions mainly in antifungal defense in barnyardgrass.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Defense against blast fungus and other biotic stresses.
